author: @simona625
text:
I like the comments of you being too honest or have morals. It's so very easy to find YouTubers who are the opposite, and do not care about those who are conned into believing.

I love your videos, the content being a little short sometimes, still conveys so much information, and you don't admit to knowing everything, which is great, because no-one can know everything, and those that claim they do, can not really be believed unfortunately. I also love it when you laugh when something works, as though you never expect it to. The enthusiasm is infectious.

I have a number of your designs, which I have yet to print and build, but I am at a loss as to where to start. Also I'm short on funds for filament.

I know it is an absolute cheek, but I would, if possible, like to be considered when you decide to give away any of your review products. They would be given a good home and allow me to progress (when I have the funds of cause). Thank you

author: @VeniceInventors
text:
After all the church needs have been addressed, another good place for donating the devices would be some maker space, if there's one in your area. It would be good if it's a space open to anyone, not some commercial overcharging place as those can afford to buy their own hardware. 
Here in Los Angeles, a city with at least 8 million people, there are surprisingly only a dozen maker spaces, and they all are on the same side of town. Maybe it's still too new a concept, I don't know, but I wish every neighborhood had one as I think they provide a great opportunity for people to grow their skills and creativity.

Only had $5000 advertised.
So I turned a few buyers down until I was satisfied 😢, with the buyers. Then I let them have it for $1000. 
Hahahaha, had to block one of the other buyers from phone. Anyway it is working out okay. 70 years old and going on holiday to visit Asia, to places I haven't been. Yeah, come from English stock, but born in Australia. Good luck to you and have a happy and prosperous life. 👍❤️😇

author: @Alkimi
text: I don't think you should feel bad about selling the products. You can think of them as alternative forms of payment or bribe attempts. If someone sends you money and says, "take this money, I hope it helps, and please vote for me for City Councilor" then they're just giving you money in the hopes that it will ingratiate you to them enough that you'll help them out, but it's effectively a bribe, not a purchase or an official exchange of goods until & unless you agree to sponsor THEM in exchange for the goods. Agreement comes first, then the exchange, and your payment is yours to liquidate as you wish.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: We all view things differently mate - I can understand what you are saying - I just don't agree with it - but that doesn't make what you are saying wrong - it's just not for me. I see it as an opportunity to get hold of equipment I couldn't afford to buy. Then I can explore it for a while share what I learn, what I think and pass the equipment on to others that can make better use of it. I don't believe I own the equipment - it's just for me to use for a short time then hand on.

replies.author: @Alkimi
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering that's a great perspective to have in general

author: @Alkimi
text: Very much appreciate hearing this info. I've been trying to figure out how I feel about advertising myself lately and I always wondered how often people get offers to sponsor products in exchange for the product.

replies.author: @Alkimi

replies.author: @totherarf
replies.text:
@mikaelfransson3658 Mikael, you are probably unaware of the Kafkaesque nature of getting a turbine up on your property in the UK. 

There is Planning Permission where you have to have your plans approved by a council who do not want turbines in their towns. 
Then you have the Suppliers and DNO's who also do not want you to have a turbine up. 
Then there is the fact that the vast majority of UK properties are not suitable to house one eather because of the room required or the aspect of the property. Next we have the cost of having a "suitable" system, with battery and inverter to supply your home with and the inefficiency of the VAWT meaning you would need a substantial one to have a hope of breaking near even in energy generation (assuming you go off grid) ..... if you stay grid connected you are limited to 2KW of generation by your DNO. 

If you are on a farm you might find it a good way to go, but there are few of us that are in that situation!
Please do not misunderstand ....... I would Love to put a turbine on my house to boost my solar, but it is not feasible in our current situation!
Rob has been looking at micro generation which is more practical in our situation here!

Selling them is often frowned upon and or restricted on many continued partnerships.

Best of luck hopefully the church benefits greatly from your generosity ❤.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it stung if i am honest - lol - and I hope it does help

author: @aleksandar7393
text: Hi Rob, here is idea, I do not know how big is the place where you live, but why not creating a engineer club, you can appoint someone to manage it, and you can donate all of your spare things, and also ask other people to donate tools, it can be good place where young people can learn about inventing and business, also it is quite possible that if you give idea to your council probably they could help with space, and also give you donations as it would be non-profit and educational purpose. In that way you could inspire new generation of inventors, and from your part it would require minimum time and effort. What do you say?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: That is an awesome idea mate - cheers

replies.author: @j.christie2594
replies.text: Brilliant

author: @lewiscobb7817
text: An excellent approach and one that I am sure the church would very much appreciate.  Your little church reminds me of a place in England that I visited once over 45 years ago as a high school student. It's in Colerne I think.  My uncle who was a pilot for the RAF and from Newfoundland, Canada is buried there.  That's way back before Newfoundland was a part of Canada.  Anyway, keep up the good work and cheers from the east coast of Canada !

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Nice story mate - thank you for sharing

replies.author: @l0I0I0I0
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering  Just keep doing what your doing!  U got my support!  I'm still studying accounting and its going well!  I'm a master at excel of you ever need it programmed or automated.

author: @steve29836
text: For me the competitions weren't about the prize, they were about supporting you, the channel, the community and having a platform to share ideas. Maybe in the future you could run a competition without a physical prize—just a shoutout or video acknowledgment from you!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Oh - that's a good idea mate - I liked the competitions for exactly the reasons you say - it was just great to see what folks were doing and be able to share it - I figured there would be no interest without a prize - you have certainly made me question that - hmmm lol

replies.author: @elvendragonhammer5433
replies.text: ​@ThinkingandTinkering I can't pretend to know how it works outside the US, but here, anything you send to someone else (regardless of almost any receiving country outside of Asia) can be sent "Certified Shipping" which forces the recipient to sign for the package so that the sender has proof it was delivered. It is a bit more costly, & the cost does depend on the items weight, but in general it's not much more expensive than it is to UPS/Fed EX Anyway. Here it's mostly used for bills/invoices or payments to credit card or Gas/Water/Electric providers or companies that have a history of trying to extort you for $$ you already paid or make it very difficult to cancel the service you originally signed up for. As an example, both Planet Fitness & HERTZ Rent-A-Car is absolutely notorious for it.

replies.author: @elvendragonhammer5433
replies.text: ​@ThinkingandTinkering  I can't pretend to know how it works outside the US, but here, anything you send to someone else (regardless of almost any receiving country outside of Asia) can be sent "Certified Shipping" which forces the recipient to sign for the package so that the sender has proof it was delivered. It is a bit more costly, & the cost does depend on the items weight, but in general it's not much more expensive than it is to UPS/Fed EX Anyway. Here it's mostly used for bills/invoices or payments to credit card or Gas/Water/Electric providers or companies that have a history of trying to extort you for $$ you already paid or make it very difficult to cancel the service you originally signed up for. As an example, both Planet Fitness & HERTZ Rent-A-Car is absolutely notorious for it. Perhaps there is something similar that you could use if you ever decide to do something like that again, or need something like that for business. And as always, have a good day!

author: @Squashypigeon
text: Great approach, schools are worthy recipients. Have you ever heard of or thought about donating suitable items to "Man Clubs", by which I mean small maker spaces which are formed to combat loneliness in older men etc. they get very little in the way of funding.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I have donated stuff to maker spaces mate - but we don't have a lot of them here in the UK

author: @StevenWebb
text: I think you're the perfect engineer does help solve my problem. I'm a paralysed tetraplegic, over the last few years I have been evolving a device that I can enter my own leg bag. But, I am struggling with the third version that I need to kink a silicon tube all the time, and then un-kink hit when when I send a 12 V signal to either a magnet or something.  I could upload a YouTube video of what I've done so far?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: go for it mate - you can't post the link but if you let me know it is up I can jump to your channel and find the vid

replies.author: @StevenWebb
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Recorded and uploaded while having man flu! I'm impressed with myself. Thank you for motivating me and replying to my message.
